title: Operator orchestration surface for production model swarms
origin: Authored as a missing MT-203 follow-on contract. MT-203 preserves: 'MT-205 Tauri swarm commands + SwarmControlRoom UI + operator chat box + model spin-up (local+cloud)'. Operator-selected scope confirms production factory/app wiring/operator orchestration as an in-scope follow-on.
goal: Expose the production SwarmCoordinator and MT-204 factory through quiet Tauri commands, a durable live board, and an operator orchestration/chat box that can spawn, inspect, cancel, and coordinate local and cloud model sessions without relying on hidden chat context or fragile screen reading.

base_scope.summary: Build the operator-facing orchestration path over the production coordinator: command API, live board read-model, operator chat box, model spin-up controls, and deterministic receipts.
included: Add production Tauri commands for swarm spawn, cancel, snapshot, session detail, board snapshot, board event subscription, and operator instruction submission.
Wire SwarmControlRoom to the live board read-model using typed SwarmEvent deltas plus full-snapshot reconcile fallback.
Add or complete OperatorChat as an orchestration input surface that submits bounded, attributable operator directives to the coordinator instead of free-floating prompt text.
Support local and cloud model spin-up from one form/control path with visible runtime_binding, provider, isolation_tier, worktree_id, budget, and ForceLocal/escalation policy.
Create receipts for operator actions: spawn requested, admitted, rejected, canceled, escalated, completed, failed, and reclaimed.
Keep all automation quiet: no foreground windows, no keyboard focus steal, bounded background tasks.
non_goals: No new backend factory implementation beyond invoking MT-204 surfaces.
No local->cloud routing policy changes beyond exposing MT-206 policy choices and statuses.
No persistent warm VM implementation; MT-207 owns that.
No generic marketing/landing page; the first surface is the actual control room.

expected_diff_shape: Product implementation should touch app/src-tauri swarm commands/state, app/src/components/swarm controls, board/read-model hooks, operator chat components, IPC bindings, CSS/layout tests, and focused Rust/TS tests. The governance repair only authors this contract.
reuse: MT-203 SwarmCoordinator lifecycle and SwarmEvent stream.
MT-204 ProductionModelSessionFactory and app-state coordinator wiring.
VM_WORKTREE_SWARM_RESEARCH_BASIS board research: Vibe Kanban snapshot+live-delta pattern, typed Tauri app.emit events, seq/epoch reconcile, hand-rolled CSS grid.
Existing SwarmControlRoom, OperatorChat, BroadcastSwarmSink, FanoutSwarmSink, DurableSwarmFrBridge, and kernel_swarm_board_snapshot surfaces recorded in the research basis.
Existing Flight Recorder, process ledger, session checkpoint, and grouping keys for observable operator actions.
gaps_closed: Closes the gap between backend swarm capability and an operator-usable orchestration surface.
Closes UI polling drift by using push events with snapshot reconciliation.
Closes hidden prompt/control ambiguity by turning operator chat actions into typed commands with receipts.
Closes parallel model workflow confusion by showing grouping, ownership, provider, state, budget, and teardown status on one board.
Makes later local->cloud escalation review cheaper by showing route decisions in the same session timeline.

high_roi_additions.item: Board drill-down panel with session timeline, ledger rows, FR events, and latest error fingerprint
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: The board already has session cards; a drill-down reuses existing query surfaces and prevents context loss during debugging.
high_roi_additions.addresses: Operator/LLM confusion when a card moves to Failed or Escalated without enough evidence.
high_roi_additions.reuse: SessionAggregateQueries, Flight Recorder, process ledger, SwarmEvent metadata.
high_roi_additions.validation: Component test opens a card and sees ordered lifecycle rows without overlapping text.

high_roi_additions.item: Command receipts copied into session detail and FR
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Cheap when adding commands, and it makes multi-agent handoff recoverable.
high_roi_additions.addresses: Lost attribution for who spawned/canceled/escalated a model.
high_roi_additions.reuse: Existing receipt and FR event patterns.
high_roi_additions.validation: Spawn/cancel tests assert receipt_id appears in Tauri response, board card, and FR payload.

high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: Reuses MT-206 routing policy in dry-run mode and prevents wrong provider/isolation choices.
high_roi_additions.addresses: Unexpected cloud spend or data egress when the operator intended local-only.
high_roi_additions.validation: UI test toggles ForceLocal and verifies cloud options become disabled and explained by typed state, not prose.

high_roi_additions.item: Visual debug snapshot command for the board
high_roi_additions.why_high_roi: The GUI is already being touched and global rules require model-usable visual diagnostics.
high_roi_additions.addresses: Future LLMs cannot judge board usability or state without screenshots/state dumps.
high_roi_additions.reuse: Existing visual debug and inspector patterns.
high_roi_additions.validation: Playwright or available browser smoke captures desktop/mobile board states and checks no text overlap.

risks_and_concerns.risk: UI commands bypass coordinator admission controls.
risks_and_concerns.plausible_failure_scenario: A button directly creates a model runtime or cloud process without the MT-203/MT-204 budget, lease, and breaker path.
risks_and_concerns.hardening: All UI commands call a single Tauri command layer that delegates to the coordinator; direct runtime/factory calls are banned outside that layer.
risks_and_concerns.verification: Static scan and tests assert commands invoke coordinator methods and admission rejections surface as typed UI states.

risks_and_concerns.risk: Live board drifts from backend truth.
risks_and_concerns.plausible_failure_scenario: Broadcast lag drops events and a session remains visually Ready after backend cancellation.
risks_and_concerns.hardening: Use seq/epoch deltas, resync-on-gap, periodic full-snapshot safety net, and visible stale/resync indicator.
risks_and_concerns.verification: Injected dropped-event test asserts board requests snapshot and corrects card state.

risks_and_concerns.risk: Operator chat becomes an unbounded prompt pipe.
risks_and_concerns.plausible_failure_scenario: Free text causes hidden, unattributed model actions or embeds secrets into logs.
risks_and_concerns.hardening: Parse chat into typed operator intents with bounded payload, redaction, receipt_id, and explicit target swarm/session.
risks_and_concerns.verification: Tests reject oversized/directive-ambiguous input and assert redaction in logs/FR.

risks_and_concerns.risk: GUI work steals focus or opens foreground windows during model operation.
risks_and_concerns.plausible_failure_scenario: Starting a local/cloud model opens a terminal or provider CLI window.
risks_and_concerns.hardening: Use quiet background process rules, hidden child process creation, and in-app terminal/output capture only when explicitly requested.
risks_and_concerns.verification: Process-spawn tests assert CREATE_NO_WINDOW/quiet flags where applicable and no shell open calls in swarm commands.

risks_and_concerns.risk: Control room is unreadable under many parallel sessions.
risks_and_concerns.plausible_failure_scenario: N=8 sessions with long IDs overlap columns or hide important state.
risks_and_concerns.hardening: Stable grid dimensions, truncation with tooltips, responsive columns, grouped swimlanes, and no nested cards.
risks_and_concerns.verification: Desktop/mobile visual smoke with N=8 fixture checks readability, navigation, controls, and no overlap.

implementation_notes: Use typed IPC DTOs; avoid raw serde_json::Value at the Tauri/frontend boundary except for explicitly versioned metadata bags.
Board actions must be commands, not direct state mutations; the board is a read-model over coordinator truth.
OperatorChat must write explicit intent records with target swarm/session and receipt correlation.
Expose ForceLocal/escalation policy controls but leave policy semantics to MT-206.
Do not create marketing/hero UI; the control room is the primary first screen for this feature.
validator_focus: All spawn/cancel/escalate actions route through the coordinator and produce receipts.
Board push+deltas reconcile correctly after dropped events.
OperatorChat creates typed, attributable instructions and rejects ambiguous/oversized inputs.
UI remains readable at N=8 with desktop and mobile viewport evidence.
No foreground process/window behavior is introduced.

lifecycle.readiness_note: Do not mark READY_FOR_VALIDATION without a proper checklist receipt, command proof, and visual evidence.

governance_notes: Governance repair 2026-06-02 reconciled packet.json so MT-205 is declared as a current follow-on contract blocked by MT-204.
packet.json keeps MT-204 as the active/claimable next follow-on; MT-205 remains PENDING until MT-204 reaches READY_FOR_VALIDATION or a later operator-directed sequencing change occurs.
The visible governance worktree has only a small app source subset; implementation must be verified against the product worktree.

minimum_controls: No UI path bypasses coordinator admission.
Operator directives are typed, attributable, and bounded.
Visual smoke evidence covers high-card-count board states.
